Leveraging geological expertise, disciplined exploration and strategic project development, the company targets meaningful growth and long-term value creation for its shareholders within the gold exploration sector.

data.title: Max Power to Drill Second Natural Hydrogen Well in Saskatchewan as Expansion Accelerates
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Max Power Mining Corp ( (TSE:MAXX) ) has issued an update.

Max Power Mining Corp. is expanding its Saskatchewan natural hydrogen program by preparing to drill a second exploration well, “Bracken,” on its Grasslands Project near the Saskatchewan‑Montana border, following the confirmed natural hydrogen discovery at the Lawson prospect on the Genesis Trend. Underpinned by newly acquired proprietary 2D seismic data and the company’s MAXX LEMI AI platform, Bracken will test a different geological play concept from Lawson and is intended to demonstrate basin‑scale continuity and the repeatability of natural hydrogen systems; success would help de‑risk additional targets across Max Power’s extensive land package and accelerate its push toward potential commercialization and a leading position in this emerging clean energy niche.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:MAXX) stock is a Hold with a C$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Max Power Mining Corp stock, see the TSE:MAXX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:MAXX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:MAXX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing losses, and sustained cash burn), partially offset by a debt-free balance sheet. Technicals are supportive due to strong trend vs. moving averages, but overbought momentum indicators increase near-term risk. Valuation is difficult to support given negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:MAXX stock, click here.

More about Max Power Mining Corp

Max Power Mining Corp. is a Canadian resource company that has positioned itself as an early mover in the emerging natural hydrogen sector in Saskatchewan. The company controls what it describes as Canada’s largest permitted land package for natural hydrogen, anchored by the 475‑km‑long Genesis Trend and the 75‑km‑wide Grasslands Project near the Saskatchewan‑Montana border, and is developing proprietary AI‑assisted exploration tools to target scalable, low‑carbon energy resources.

Average Trading Volume: 860,054

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$98.92M

data.title: Saybrook Capital /Nc trims Alphabet Inc. Class A stake
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Saybrook Capital /Nc, managed by Luke M. Babcock& Scott R. Hirsch, recently executed a significant transaction involving Alphabet Inc. Class A ((GOOGL)). The hedge fund reduced its position by 1,048 shares.

Recent Updates on Alphabet Inc. Class A stock

In recent weeks, Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) has continued a strong rally, rising roughly 4–6% over the past week, about 8–9% over the last month, and around 74–78% over the past year, significantly outperforming the NASDAQ and its FANG peers in 2025. Wall Street maintains a Strong Buy consensus, with 12‑month average price targets in the high $330s to about $340, implying modest further upside from recent closes near $332–$336. Bank of America analyst Justin Post reaffirmed his Buy rating on January 13, 2026, with a $370 price objective, positioning Alphabet as a top vehicle for AI exposure thanks to its Gemini large language model, in‑house TPU infrastructure, massive Search reach, and growing Cloud and Workspace adoption. He sees additional upside from better monetization of AI‑enhanced search, faster Google Cloud growth, emerging “Agentic AI” opportunities, and improving commercialization of TPUs and Other Bets, while cautioning that intensifying AI and search competition, rising AI costs, potential cloud overcapacity, slower ad growth, and Alphabet’s elevated valuation could pressure margins and sentiment. Overall, the risk/reward is viewed as balanced but still attractive given expected earnings growth and Alphabet’s leading AI position.

Spark’s Take on GOOGL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GOOGL is a Outperform.

Alphabet’s strong financial performance and positive earnings call are the most significant factors driving the stock score. Technical analysis supports a bullish outlook, though valuation concerns slightly temper the overall score. Strategic investments in AI and cloud services position Alphabet well for future growth, despite some cost-related challenges.

To see Spark’s full report on GOOGL stock, click here.

More about Alphabet Inc. Class A

YTD Price Performance: 8.07%

First Hydrogen ( (TSE:FHYD) ) just unveiled an update.

First Hydrogen Corp. has closed a private placement financing of 5.2 million units, raising gross proceeds of $1.56 million, with each unit comprising one common share and a warrant exercisable at $0.45 for two years. Conducted under a listed issuer financing exemption that removes hold-period restrictions, the raise provides fresh working capital and general corporate funding, while the company also paid a $124,800 cash finder’s fee and issued 416,000 finder warrants on the same terms, modestly strengthening its balance sheet to support ongoing development of its zero-emission vehicle and green hydrogen initiatives.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:FHYD) stock is a Hold with a C$0.41 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on First Hydrogen stock, see the TSE:FHYD Stock Forecast page.

Valuation provides limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend yield, while recent corporate events are modestly positive but do not offset the core financial and trend risks.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:FHYD stock, click here.

More about First Hydrogen

First Hydrogen Corp. is a Vancouver-, Montreal-, Germany- and London-based company focused on zero-emission vehicles and green hydrogen production and distribution. It has designed and built two hydrogen fuel-cell-powered light commercial vehicles that are road-legal in the UK (excluding Northern Ireland), have completed around 6,000 km of testing, and demonstrated a range of more than 630 km on a single refuelling in real-world fleet trials.

data.title: Permex Petroleum Hit With Debenture Default Notice and Missed Financial Filing Deadline
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Permex Petroleum Corporation ( (TSE:OIL) ) has provided an announcement.

Permex Petroleum Corporation has received a Notice of Default from holders of its secured convertible debentures, totaling approximately $4.28 million in principal with 10% annual interest, after failing to make required payments by November 2, 2025. The debentureholders, represented by Jeffrey E. Eberwein, have demanded immediate repayment and indicated they may enforce their security, potentially repossessing company assets if satisfactory payment or arrangements are not made by February 18, 2026. Separately, Permex has also failed to file its audited annual financial statements and related regulatory certifications for the year ended September 30, 2025, by the prescribed deadline, signaling heightened financial and regulatory pressures that could affect its liquidity, operational continuity, and standing with investors and regulators.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:OIL) stock is a Hold with a C$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Permex Petroleum Corporation stock, see the TSE:OIL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:OIL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:OIL is a Neutral.

Permex Petroleum Corporation faces significant financial challenges, with declining revenue and profitability, increased leverage, and liquidity issues being the most impactful factors. Technical analysis provides mixed signals, and poor valuation metrics further weigh down the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:OIL stock, click here.

More about Permex Petroleum Corporation

Permex Petroleum Corporation is an oil and gas company active in the energy sector, with its securities listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange and the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. The company’s operations are backed by secured financing arrangements, including convertible debentures collateralized by its assets.

Helix BioPharma Corp. reported that shareholders overwhelmingly approved all matters at its latest annual general and special meeting, with more than 81% of outstanding shares voted and all four director nominees re-elected by near-unanimous support. Investors also endorsed the reappointment of Clearhouse LLP as auditor and authorized a share consolidation of up to five existing shares for one new share at the board’s discretion, a move that could give the company greater flexibility in managing its capital structure and market positioning as it advances its oncology pipeline.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:HBP stock, click here.

More about Helix BioPharma

Helix BioPharma Corp. is a clinical-stage oncology company focused on developing novel therapies for hard-to-treat solid cancers, leveraging a proprietary bio-conjugate platform targeting CEACAM6-expressing tumors. Its lead candidate, L-DOS47, an antibody-enzyme conjugate designed to modify the tumor microenvironment and enhance anti-cancer immunity, has completed Phase Ib trials in non-small cell lung cancer, with additional bi-specific antibody-drug conjugates in discovery.

data.title: Nokia Earnings Call: AI Bets Offset Margin Pressures
data.description:
Nokia ((NOK)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nokia Earnings Call Balances AI-Driven Upside With Near-Term Margin Strain

Nokia’s latest earnings call struck a tone of cautious optimism, as management highlighted modest top-line growth, strong momentum in high-value Network Infrastructure and AI & cloud segments, and a solid balance sheet, while openly flagging near-term margin pressure from heavy investment, acquisitions and restructuring. The company framed 2025–2026 as a transition period: short-term profitability is under strain from integration costs, product ramps and regional headwinds, but management argued that these moves are laying the foundation for stronger multi-year growth and margin expansion.

Steady Revenue Growth and Solid 2025 Profit Delivery

Nokia reported Q4 net sales of EUR 6.1 billion, up 3% year over year, capping 2025 full-year net sales at EUR 19.9 billion. Full-year operating profit reached EUR 2.0 billion, slightly above the midpoint of guidance, providing some reassurance that the company can navigate a complex demand environment while investing heavily for future growth. The numbers underscore a business that is not in hyper-growth mode but is steadily expanding, with profitability holding up reasonably well despite product and portfolio transitions.

Cash Generation Remains a Key Strength

The company delivered Q4 free cash flow of EUR 226 million and a full-year 2025 free cash flow conversion of 72%, squarely within the guided 50%–80% range. Nokia ended the quarter with net cash of EUR 3.4 billion, including a EUR 0.9 billion equity investment in NVIDIA, underpinning its ability to fund capex, acquisitions and restructuring. For investors, the strong net cash position and consistent cash conversion help buffer the earnings volatility expected from ongoing portfolio repositioning and integration programs.

Network Infrastructure and Optical Networks Drive Growth

Network Infrastructure was a clear bright spot, with net sales up 7% in Q4 and Optical Networks particularly strong at 17% growth. The segment’s book-to-bill was above 1, indicating demand is outpacing reported revenue and building a healthy backlog. Management tied much of this strength to AI and cloud-related demand, positioning Network Infrastructure as a core engine of Nokia’s medium-term growth story and a counterweight to softer mobile-related markets.

AI & Cloud Customers Becoming a Growth Engine

AI and cloud customers now represent a meaningful share of Nokia’s business, accounting for 16% of Q4 net sales and 30% of Optical Networks revenue. The company reported EUR 2.4 billion of orders from AI & cloud customers in 2025, illustrating that cloud service providers and AI data-center players are becoming central to the growth thesis. This diversification away from traditional telecom operators supports a structurally higher growth profile but also demands significant upfront investment in capacity and technology.

Optical Product Ramps Back the AI Narrative

Nokia’s 800G ZR and ZR+ pluggable optical products are now shipping, with initial field performance described as positive, and the company has secured multiple design wins and is supplying into scale deployments. The near-term focus is on ramping production, which is capital-intensive but critical to capturing the wave of AI and cloud network upgrades. Successful execution in this ramp will be important for translating the strong order book into sustained revenue and margin expansion.

IP Networking and Automation Push Into Data Centers

In IP networking, Nokia launched the 7220 IXR-H6 data center switching platform and introduced Agentic AI event-driven automation, which it claims can reduce network downtime by up to 96%. The company also secured a design win for its next-generation data center switching platform, signaling growing relevance in cloud-scale data centers. These advances indicate that Nokia aims to compete not just in transport, but also in the data-center fabric and automation layers that are critical to AI workloads.

Strategic Acquisitions and New Units Reshape the Portfolio

The completion of the Infinera acquisition is central to Nokia’s strategy to scale and deepen its optical capabilities, particularly for AI and cloud traffic. At the same time, the company has reorganized into a new Mobile Infrastructure segment encompassing Core Software, Radio Networks and Technology Standards, and launched a Nokia Defense incubation unit to pursue defense and NATO-related opportunities. These moves signal a push into higher-value, more defensible segments while streamlining how the core telecom and networking assets are managed.

Full Ownership of Nokia Shanghai Bell and Synergy Plan

Nokia has closed the transaction to take full ownership of Nokia Shanghai Bell (NSB), aiming to unlock around EUR 200 million in run-rate cost synergies over 24–36 months. However, the integration will not be cheap, with expected costs of EUR 350–400 million over that period. Investors should expect a meaningful gap between comparable and reported earnings as these integration expenses are absorbed, but the company argues the structural cost benefits will enhance competitiveness and margins over the longer term.

Ambitious 2026 and 2028 Profitability Targets

Management laid out clear multi-year financial targets, including a 2026 operating profit range of EUR 2.0–2.5 billion. For Network Infrastructure, Nokia is targeting a 6%–8% CAGR in net sales from 2025–2028, rising to 10%–12% for the combined Optical and IP businesses, with operating margins aiming for 13%–17% by 2028 versus 9.5% in 2025. Mobile Infrastructure is targeted to deliver at least EUR 1.5 billion in operating profit, underlining the company’s expectation that this segment stabilizes and contributes solidly even as growth is driven elsewhere.

Core Software and Standards Maintain Commercial Traction

Within Core Software, Nokia highlighted a notable 5G core win with Telia and continued expansion of its Network as Code platform, which now counts over 75 partners, including 43 telecom operators. Meanwhile, the Technology Standards business maintains a contracted net sales run rate of around EUR 1.4 billion, providing a relatively steady and high-margin revenue base. These activities underscore that Nokia’s software and licensing assets continue to support the broader group, even as other segments undergo more volatility.

Investments and Ramps Squeeze Operating Margins

Despite the growth pockets, Q4 operating margin slipped to 17.3%, 90 basis points lower than a year earlier. Management attributed this to stepped-up investments in growth areas, including Infinera-related costs, and the margin drag from ramping new products that initially ship at lower profitability. While investors may be wary of the near-term compression, the company framed this as a deliberate trade-off to capture long-term growth in AI, cloud and optical.

Network Infrastructure Margins Hit by Costs and Mix

Within Network Infrastructure, gross margin declined by 80 basis points to 44.6% in Q4, with operating margin further pressured by increased R&D spending and acquisition-related costs. The margin deterioration reflects both the cost of building out next-generation products and the mix effect of rapid growth in areas that are still maturing from a profitability perspective. Nokia is effectively front-loading costs in a segment it expects to be the cornerstone of its medium-term margin expansion.

Nokia Technologies Sees Revenue Drop and Impairment

Nokia Technologies, the company’s licensing and intellectual property arm, posted a 17% year-over-year decline in Q4 net sales and recorded a EUR 20 million impairment against a prior asset purchase. This underlines that the business is not immune to volatility and contract timing and that it cannot be relied upon as a consistently growing profit pool. While still a valuable contributor, the quarter’s performance reinforces management’s shift in focus toward infrastructure and cloud-driven growth engines.

Cloud & Network Services Affected by Timing, Not Trend

Cloud and Network Services (CNS) revenue declined 4% in Q4, but the company emphasized that this was driven by revenue recognition phasing rather than underlying weakness, with full-year growth of 6%. Gross margin in the quarter benefited from a one-off EUR 37 million provision reversal, flattered by this non-recurring item. Investors should view CNS as a steadily growing but somewhat lumpy business where quarterly swings can be misleading relative to the full-year trajectory.

Fixed Networks Flat as Lower-Margin Products Are Deprioritized

Fixed Networks delivered flat performance in Q4 overall, even though fiber OLT sales grew 16% year over year. The muted top-line outcome reflects a deliberate choice by Nokia to deprioritize certain CPE products that dilute margins. This indicates a continued focus on quality of revenue and margin protection rather than chasing volume in commoditized hardware, a theme that fits with the company’s broader portfolio discipline.

Restructuring and Integration Costs Weigh on Reported Numbers

Nokia flagged around EUR 300 million of restructuring in 2025 and guided roughly EUR 450 million of cash outflow for restructuring in 2026, alongside NSB integration costs of EUR 350–400 million over 24–36 months. These cash drains and P&L impacts will create a notable gap between reported and comparable earnings, complicating the near-term financial picture. Nonetheless, management argues that these programs are essential to support long-term cost competitiveness, scale and margin targets.

Seasonality and Q1 2026 Headwinds Loom

Management cautioned that Q1 2026 net sales are expected to fall somewhat more than the historical average sequential decline of around 24% (excluding Nokia Technologies), due to above-normal activity in Q4 2025. This guidance sets the stage for a weak first quarter headline, even if the underlying demand trends remain intact. Investors should therefore expect a soft start to the year, with the company asking the market to look past near-term seasonality to the stronger structural outlook.

Mobile Infrastructure Faces Regional and Contract Pressure

The Mobile Infrastructure segment continues to battle top-line headwinds linked to prior contract losses, particularly in North America tied to AT&T. Management also cited regional declines in India and Greater China, and noted that both CNS and Mobile Networks saw declines in North America during the quarter. While mobile remains a core business, the call made clear that growth in the near term will be uneven and heavily influenced by regional and customer-specific dynamics.

Supply Chain and Manufacturing Scale a Key Execution Risk

Nokia acknowledged ongoing supply constraints across the AI and data-center ecosystem and highlighted the need to scale optical manufacturing capacity, including higher capex and ramping new fabrication capacity. This introduces a tangible execution risk: if ramp-up timing lags demand, the company may not fully capture near-term revenue opportunities despite strong orders. Investors will be watching closely for progress on capacity expansions to ensure the AI and cloud opportunity translates into realized sales.

Wide Guidance Range Reflects Margin and Investment Uncertainty

The 2026 operating profit guidance of EUR 2.0–2.5 billion carries a relatively wide EUR 500 million range, underscoring uncertainty around the profitability impact of new product launches and AI & cloud investments. Management highlighted potential downward pressure on gross margin and operating expenses as these growth areas scale. While the top end of the range implies meaningful margin improvement, the breadth of the guidance is a reminder that the transition carries execution and market risks.

Forward-Looking Guidance: Growth in Infrastructure, Transition Elsewhere

Looking ahead, Nokia guided 2026 operating profit of EUR 2.0–2.5 billion and expects Network Infrastructure growth in 2026 to track its 2025–2028 CAGR targets of 6%–8% overall and 10%–12% for Optical and IP combined. The company aims to lift Network Infrastructure operating margins toward 13%–17% by 2028 from 9.5% in 2025, while Mobile Infrastructure is targeted to deliver at least EUR 1.5 billion in operating profit in 2026, roughly in line with 2025. Portfolio Businesses are expected to reduce their operating loss from 2025’s EUR 97 million, and Group Common costs are forecast to fall to about EUR 150 million from EUR 190 million. Key modelling assumptions include positive comparable net financial income, a tax rate around 26%–27% with about EUR 500 million of cash tax outflows, capex of EUR 900–1,000 million mainly to expand optical manufacturing, and free cash flow conversion of 65%–75%. Management also flagged FX sensitivity, expected Q1 2026 seasonality-driven revenue declines, and NSB integration synergies of roughly EUR 200 million at run rate against integration costs of EUR 350–400 million over 24–36 months.

Nokia’s earnings call painted the picture of a company mid-transition: leveraging strong cash generation and a fortified balance sheet to invest aggressively in AI, cloud, optical and defense while digesting acquisitions and weathering mobile and regional headwinds. The overall sentiment leaned positive, with clear multi-year growth and margin targets and strong demand indicators in Network Infrastructure and AI-related segments, even as near-term margins, restructuring costs and Q1 seasonality temper the outlook. For investors, the story now hinges on execution—scaling manufacturing, integrating acquisitions and delivering on cost synergy and margin expansion promises over the next several years.
